steps.title: Establish the pay structure
steps.content: For the first 40 hours, the employee earns $30/hr, yielding 40 × $30 = $1,200. For each hour beyond 40, the rate is $45/hr. The goal is at least \$1,610.

steps.title: Backsolve the candidates
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (49): The employee works 49 − 40 = 9 hours of overtime at $45/hr, yielding $1,200 + 9($45) = $1,200 + $405 = $1,605. Since \$1,605 < \$1,610, B fails.

Testing choice A (50): The employee works 50 − 40 = 10 hours of overtime at $45/hr, yielding $1,200 + 10($45) = $1,200 + $450 = $1,650. Since \$1,650 ≥ \$1,610, the target is met. That matches choice A — A is correct.
